target_text:
The most frustrating part of building AI agents isn't the model. It's that they go half blind the moment they touch the web.

Every platform has its own way of handling content, search, and authentication.

I built ReachKit so agents can read URLs, search across platforms,
quote: the web scraping problem is worse than auth or format differences. most sites actively break programmatic access on purpose and you end up maintaining a fleet of browser instances just to read text that's already public

target_text:
People often talk about AI capabilities.

Faster reasoning Better automation Smarter agents

But I think we’re overlooking something just as important

How do we know an AI agent deserves our trust?

A successful outcome isn’t enough.

Any system can get something right once. https://t.co/7TVMWZc2c0
quote: trust breaks differently than capability. an llm that hallucinates a citation is annoying. an agent that quietly spends your money wrong or deletes the right file for the wrong reason is worse. repeatability matters more than accuracy once.
